name: Lemon-Cheese Babka
recipeInstructions:
Cheese Filling:
8 ounces cream cheese
1/2 cup Cottage cheese
1 Egg yolk
1/4 cup Sugar
1 teaspoon Grated lemon rind
Crumb Topping:
3 tablespoons Flour
1/3 cup Chopped nuts
3 tablespoons Butter or margarine (softened)
3 tablespoons Sugar
1/4 teaspoon Ground cinnamon
1. Sprinkle yeast and 1/2 teaspoon of sugar over warm water in a 1-cup measure. Stir to dissolve yeast. Let stand until bubbly, about 10 minutes.
2. Combine 2 cups flour, salt, lemon rind, and remaining sugar in a large bowl; make a well in the middle. Beat eggs and egg yolks in a small bowl just to mix. Pour into the well with liquids. Stir liquids into flour until smooth. Add butter gradually, beating well. Stir in 1 more cup of flour. Beat until dough leaves side of bowl.
3. Knead on a lightly floured surface; until smooth and elastic, about 8 minutes.
4. Press dough into a buttered large bowl, turning to bring buttered side up; cover. Let rise in warm place, 1 1/2 to 2 hours, or until doubled in volume.
5. While the dough is rising, make cheese filling: Beat cream cheese and cottage cheese in small bowl with electric mixer until smooth; beat in egg yolk and sugar. Stir in lemon rind.
6. Make crumb topping: Combine nuts, flour, butter, sugar, and cinnamon in a small bowl. Grease two 8 x 1 1/2 inch pans.
7. When dough has doubled, punch down, knead raisins; divide into four equal parts. Press two parts into bottoms and about 1/2 inch up sides of prepared pans; spread each with about 1 cup cheese filling.
8. Shape remaining dough into 2 8-inch circles; place on top of cheese filling. Press spoon handle around edges to seal. Sprinkle half crumb topping over each. Let rise in warm place until dough reaches tops of pans, about 1 hour.
9. Bake in preheated 350°F oven for 40 minutes, or until cakes sound hollow when tapped. Cool on wire rack. Place foil loosely over crumb topping; invert onto rack, then turn right side up. Let cool at least 30 minutes before serving. Sprinkle with powdered sugar.
